name: Security Bank and Trust Company
description_short: Community bank operating since 1922 in Oklahoma, offering checking, savings, mortgages, commercial loans, and business services across four branches.
description_long: Security Bank and Trust Company is a locally-owned community bank that has been serving the Miami, Oklahoma area and surrounding communities since 1922. The bank operates four physical branches—Main Bank in Miami, North Main Branch in Miami, Commerce Branch in Commerce, and Afton Branch in Afton—providing traditional banking services with a focus on personalized, local decision-making rather than corporate policies. The institution emphasizes its role as a community-focused lender that understands local economic conditions and customer needs. The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and commercial banking products. For individuals, they provide checking accounts, savings accounts, personal loans, credit cards, and safe deposit boxes with multiple size options ($10–$55 annually). For businesses, Security Bank and Trust offers commercial loans, business credit cards, and commercial products designed for various business types. The mortgage division provides residential mortgages and land loans with multiple financing options and terms. The bank also offers online banking, mobile banking access, and various calculators to help customers plan finances. Security Bank and Trust differentiates itself through its emphasis on local decision-making, local financing approval, and local customer service. Unlike large national banks, the institution markets itself as having direct relationships with customers and understanding the specific community context in which businesses operate. They advertise multiple branch locations for convenience, 24-hour mobile access, and a commitment to community support alongside commercial success. The company explicitly contrasts its local model against "big banks." As a traditional community bank, Security Bank and Trust serves customers who prefer local relationships and personal service over digital-first or national banking options. The website provides limited detail about specific rates, loan terms, or competitive advantages beyond the local-bank positioning. No information is available about checking account fees, minimum balances, mortgage rates, or commercial loan terms, requiring direct customer contact for specific product details. The bank's appeal depends primarily on valuing local banking relationships over lower fees or higher interest rates that online or larger institutions may offer.
pros: Four physical branch locations across Oklahoma (Miami, Commerce, Afton) for in-person banking access
Established institution serving the community since 1922 with long-term stability
24-hour mobile and online banking access alongside traditional branch services
Makes local lending decisions rather than applying corporate policies, beneficial for local business owners
Offers both personal and commercial products under one institution (checking, savings, loans, mortgages, credit cards)
Safe deposit boxes available in multiple sizes at stated annual rates ($10–$55)
Toll-free number (888-330-6419) and multiple direct branch phone lines for customer support
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Oklahoma, not viable for customers outside the region
Website lacks specific information on checking account fees, minimum balances, interest rates, or loan terms
No comparison tools or rate transparency to help customers evaluate competitiveness against regional/national competitors
Requires direct contact with loan officers for personalized quotes; no online application or instant decision options mentioned
Heavy marketing emphasis on 'local bank' positioning suggests potentially higher fees or rates than national competitors
best_for: Oklahoma business owners seeking local lending decisions and community bank relationships
Residents of Miami, Commerce, and Afton, Oklahoma who prefer in-person banking and local branch access
Customers valuing long-established institutions with community reputation over digital-only or national bank options
Small business owners and commercial customers who benefit from local decision-makers understanding their market
diagnosis: Security Bank and Trust Company is best for Oklahoma residents and business owners who prioritize local banking relationships, in-person service, and community-focused lending over competitive rates or digital convenience. The main caveat is the lack of pricing transparency on the website—customers must contact the bank directly to compare terms, fees, and rates against regional and national competitors before committing.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
